rationale: Target is a 4-unit attached townhome at 3561 E 102nd Ct in a multi-building complex in Thornton, CO. I identified the center building in the middle row as the most likely candidate. Bounding box estimated at ~200 px (E-W) × ~220 px (N-S), yielding a gross area of 44,000 sq px × 0.035318 = 1,554 sq ft, reduced ~5% for corner notches/cutouts to ~1,476 sq ft. The roof is a complex multi-section hip style (confirmed by absence of rake edges and visible hip lines at all four outer corners plus inner step-down hips where unit sections meet). Four short ridge segments (~60 px each) run along the peak of each unit's roof plane. Eight hip lines (4 outer + 4 inner) estimated at ~45 px each. Four valleys where adjacent unit sections meet estimated at ~25 px each. Eaves span the full perimeter (~158 ft). No rake edges (pure hip roof). Confidence is moderate-low because exact unit boundary within the shared building roof is uncertain, and tree shadows obscure some edge details.
